Title: Biosynthesis of Chlorophyll and Other Isoprenoids in the Plastid of Red Grape Berry Skins
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photosynthetic light harvesting; contributes to grape and wine sensory characteristics via chlorophyll metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxygenates chlorophyllide a to form chlorophyllide b in chlorophyll b biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: downregulated by 65% in berry skins at E-L 38 versus E-L 34; also downregulated in leaves during transition to E-L 38
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in green berry skins than mature berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developmental stage transition from green (E-L 34) to mature (E-L 38)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: decreased expression toward maturity is associated with reduced de novo chlorophyll biosynthesis in berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvCAO transcript abundance decreased in berry skins at maturity. 2. Its downregulation paralleled reduced expression of another chlorophyll biosynthetic gene, VvCHLG. 3. Results support decreased de novo chlorophyll biosynthesis toward E-L 38 while chloroplasts persist in red berry skins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted plastid metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted metabolomics; qPCR; untargeted metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: altered chlorophyll metabolism could affect photosynthetic machinery maintenance and berry coloration
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photosynthetic pigments; influence grape and wine sensory characteristics
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: chlorophyllide a or b; phytyl diphosphate/geranylgeranyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: esterifies chlorophyllide with phytyl or geranylgeranyl pyrophosphate to produce chlorophyll
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; discussed as preponderant influence in network regulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: downregulated by 65% in berry skins at E-L 38 versus E-L 34; also downregulated in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in green berry skins than mature berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developmental stage transition from E-L 34 to E-L 38
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduced transcript level at maturity is associated with lower de novo chlorophyll biosynthesis while chlorophyll levels remain relatively stable in berry skin plastids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvCHLG expression declined in mature berry skins. 2. The decline supports reduced chlorophyll biosynthesis during ripening. 3. VvCHLG occupied a core network position linking major transcript-metabolite clusters.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; plastid targeted metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted metabolomics; qPCR; untargeted metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: may alter berry color masking, photosystem maintenance, and downstream pigment balance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text notes esterification step seems active in skins at E-L 38 despite reduced transcript levels.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: carotenoids; chlorophyll side-chain precursors; plastid isoprenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photosynthetic pigments, antioxidants, and sensory-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: IPP/DMAPP interconversion supplying multiple plastidial isoprenoid branches
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: interconverts IPP and DMAPP to balance prenyl precursor pools for isoprenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated by 286% in berry skins at E-L 38; repressed in leaves at E-L 38 according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developmental stage transition from E-L 34 to E-L 38
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increased expression at maturity is associated with maintenance of plastid isoprenoid synthesis and positive correlation with many isoprenoids and lipids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvIPPI was strongly upregulated in mature berry skins. 2. It was a major hub positively correlated with 21 metabolites including chlorophylls, carotenoids, and lipids. 3. The data suggest enhanced prenyl precursor balancing supports isoprenoid metabolism in mature skin plastids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted plastid metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted metabolomics; qPCR; untargeted metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: broad perturbation of isoprenoid precursor pools may affect multiple branches and cause pleiotropic metabolic shifts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of the notable hub genes in the largest positive-correlation network cluster.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photosynthetic photoprotection, pigment formation, aroma and ABA precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes head-to-head condensation of two GGPP molecules to form phytoene, the precursor of all carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme; committed-step enzyme of carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: downregulated by 73% in berry skins at E-L 38; similar pattern in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in green berry skins than mature berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developmental stage transition from E-L 34 to E-L 38
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: controls carotenoid precursor formation; reduced transcript abundance during ripening coincides with altered carotenoid composition and phytoene accumulation in mature berry skin plastids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvPSY1 transcripts decreased markedly from green to mature berry skins. 2. The gene was highlighted as a key enzyme of carotenoid biosynthesis and occupied a core network position. 3. Despite lower transcript abundance, phytoene accumulated in mature skin plastids, indicating complex regulation of carotenoid flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted carotenoid metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted metabolomics; qPCR; untargeted metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: may affect multiple carotenoid-derived functions including photoprotection and hormone precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion explicitly states PSY is a key enzyme catalyzing the first committed carotenoid step and cites tomato studies.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photosynthetic photoprotection and pigment biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: introduces double bonds into phytoene during early carotenoid desaturation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: downregulated by 55% in berry skins at E-L 38
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developmental stage transition from E-L 34 to E-L 38
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduced expression at maturity is associated with carotenoid pathway reprogramming in berry skin plastids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvPDS1 expression declined in mature berry skins. 2. The decrease paralleled lower expression of VvPSY1. 3. VvPDS1 positively correlated with VvNYC1 and glycerolipids in a network subcluster.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted metabolomics; qPCR; untargeted metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: disturbance of carotenoid desaturation can impair plastid function and photoprotection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct enzyme assay reported.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor supply for pigments, antioxidants, and quinones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: synthesizes GGPP, the direct prenyl precursor for carotenoids, chlorophyll side chains, and tocopherols
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: similar abundance in green and mature berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: no significant change between E-L 34 and E-L 38 in berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: stable expression indicates maintained GGPP precursor supply during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvGGPS1 transcript levels did not differ significantly between green and mature berry skins. 2. It was highlighted as an intermediate step supplying the key metabolite phytoene. 3. In network analysis, VvGGPS1 clustered positively with genes linked to chlorophyll, tocopherol, aroma precursor, and lipid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted metabolomics; qPCR; untargeted metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: altering GGPP supply can redistribute flux among carotenoids, chlorophylls, quinones, and tocopherols
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion notes it shared common regulatory behavior with genes from multiple plastid pathways.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: electron transport and antioxidant-related quinone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: IPP; DMAPP or allylic prenyl diphosphates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: elongates prenyl chains to generate solanesyl diphosphate for plastoquinone-related biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: similar abundance in green and mature berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: no significant change between E-L 34 and E-L 38
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: stable expression suggests sustained precursor supply to quinone biosynthesis during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvSPS1 transcript abundance did not significantly change between green and mature berry skins. 2. It was categorized as an intermediate biosynthetic step connected to phytoene/related isoprenoid metabolism in the pathway schematic. 3. Data support maintenance rather than major reprogramming of this precursor step during ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; plastid metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted metabolomics; qPCR; untargeted metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: broad effects on electron transport quinones may influence photosynthetic performance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper gives limited functional detail beyond expression stability.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photoprotection and pigment formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes lycopene to generate β-ring-containing carotenoids in the β-branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; discussion suggests preponderant network influence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: similar abundance in green and mature berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: no significant change across berry ripening in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: contributes to β-carotene branch flux; expression pattern is associated with higher late-pathway carotenoids in green berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvLBCY2 showed a broadly stable transcript level from E-L 34 to E-L 38. 2. The gene was discussed together with VvLECY1 and VvVDE1 as correlating with higher late carotenoids at E-L 34. 3. VvLBCY2 occupied a core network position connecting major clusters.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted carotenoid profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted metabolomics; qPCR; untargeted metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: changing branch flux may reduce alternative ε-branch xanthophylls important for photosystems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named as a connector of network clusters in discussion.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigment formation and photoprotection
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes lycopene to introduce an ε-ring leading to ε-branch carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; discussion suggests preponderant network influence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: decreased by 76% in berry skins from E-L 34 to E-L 38
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduction at maturity is associated with lower abundance of late carotenoids and negative correlations with several isoprenoids and lipids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvLECY1 showed one of the strongest decreases among carotenoid biosynthetic genes during berry ripening. 2. It was linked to biosynthesis of δ-carotene and ε-branch carotenoids. 3. Network analysis placed VvLECY1 in a negative-correlation cluster and as a bridge among major clusters.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted carotenoid profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted metabolomics; qPCR; untargeted metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: may alter photosystem-associated xanthophyll balance and photoprotection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion highlights VvLECY1 as having preponderant influence in regulation of isoprenoid routes.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photoprotection and light harvesting
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates ε-ring-containing carotenoids in xanthophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: similar abundance in green and mature berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: stable expression suggests maintenance of late ε-branch hydroxylation capacity during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvLUT1 transcript abundance was similar in green and mature berry skins. 2. It was classified among late carotenoid pathway genes with limited developmental reprogramming. 3. Data indicate this step is not a major source of ripening-dependent variation in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; carotenoid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Limited direct discussion in paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photoprotection; xanthophyll cycle; pigment formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates β-carotene to form β-xanthophylls including zeaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: similar abundance in green and mature berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: contributes to xanthophyll biosynthesis; in network it negatively correlated with several isoprenoids and lipids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvBCH1 did not significantly change expression between green and mature berry skins. 2. It belongs to the late carotenoid pathway associated with photoprotective pigments. 3. Network analysis grouped VvBCH1 with negative correlations to several metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted carotenoid profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: may perturb xanthophyll cycle balance and stress photoprotection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The discussion cites β-carotene hydroxylase expression peaks in other cultivars as relevant background.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photoprotection through xanthophyll cycle
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: de-epoxidizes violaxanthin to produce zeaxanthin via antheraxanthin in the xanthophyll cycle
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: decreased by 64% in berry skins at E-L 38; similar pattern in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: decreased expression at maturity, together with increased VvZEP1, indicates rebalancing of the xanthophyll cycle toward epoxidation in mature berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvVDE1 expression fell strongly during berry ripening. 2. Opposite behavior of VvVDE1 and VvZEP1 suggests fine-tuned xanthophyll cycle regulation. 3. The expression pattern correlates with higher late-stage 9-cis-violaxanthin in mature berry skin plastids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted carotenoid profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: could compromise photoprotection under stress if zeaxanthin formation is reduced
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of a common expression module shared between berry skins and leaves.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photoprotection and precursor supply for ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: epoxidizes zeaxanthin to violaxanthin via antheraxanthin in the xanthophyll cycle
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate precursor of ABA branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated by 855% in berry skins at E-L 38; similar trend in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: much higher in mature berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developmental stage transition; high-light photoprotection context discussed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strong induction at maturity favors epoxidation over de-epoxidation, consistent with increased 9-cis-violaxanthin in mature berry skin plastids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvZEP1 was one of the most strongly induced genes in mature berry skins. 2. Its induction, opposite to VvVDE1, indicates fine control of the xanthophyll cycle. 3. Network analysis showed positive correlations of VvZEP1 with isoprenoids and quinones.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted carotenoid profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: shifting xanthophyll cycle too far toward epoxidation may reduce rapid non-photochemical quenching capacity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly interprets VvZEP1 induction as evidence that photoprotective mechanisms remain important at maturity.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA biosynthesis contributing to berry maturation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to generate xanthoxin, an ABA precursor
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: rate-limiting step for ABA biosynthesis discussed from prior literature; similar phenomenon suggested in grape berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: downregulated by 57% in berry skins at E-L 38
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduced expression at maturity despite higher 9-cis-violaxanthin suggests substrate accumulation and regulated ABA precursor formation during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvNCED1 expression declined during berry ripening. 2. The gene participates in ABA precursor biosynthesis from carotenoids. 3. Discussion notes NCED cleavage is rate-limiting for ABA biosynthesis in leaves and may similarly control flux in grape berry skins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted carotenoid profiling; literature-supported pathway interpretation; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: altered ABA biosynthesis may impact ripening, stress responses, and fruit quality traits broadly
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper infers rather than directly measures ABA/xanthoxin flux.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA biosynthesis contributing to berry maturation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to generate xanthoxin in ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: rate-limiting step suggested based on NCED biology
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: downregulated by 80% in berry skins at E-L 38
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strong decline at maturity indicates reduced transcript-level cleavage capacity in the ABA precursor branch despite increased 9-cis-violaxanthin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvNCED2 was strongly downregulated during berry ripening. 2. It clustered positively with multiple plastid pathway genes in network analysis. 3. Results support developmental reprogramming of the ABA precursor branch in grape skin plastids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted carotenoid profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed with VvNCED1 as part of ABA precursor regulation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: aroma and flavor compounds important for grape and wine sensory quality
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidatively cleaves carotenoids to generate volatile apocarotenoids contributing to aroma
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: did not change in berry skins between E-L 34 and E-L 38; decreased in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: stable across berry ripening in skins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: maintains potential for aroma apocarotenoid formation across berry development, though no direct metabolite flux change was shown
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvCCD1 transcript abundance remained similar in green and mature berry skins. 2. It is implicated in biosynthesis of aroma volatiles such as β-ionone. 3. Discussion notes previous transgenic evidence that VvCCD1 overexpression or silencing did not alter leaf norisoprenoid levels, suggesting additional CCD members may contribute.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; pathway/metabolite context analysis; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly notes uncertainty because transgenic manipulation in prior work did not change leaf norisoprenoids.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant protection of photosystems and membrane lipids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: downregulated during transition from E-L 34 to E-L 38 in berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduced transcript abundance accompanies quinone/tocochromanol pathway reprogramming during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvHGGT expression decreased from green to mature berry skins. 2. The gene participates in quinone/tocochromanol biosynthesis. 3. Its regulation indicates selective remodeling of antioxidant pathways during ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted quinone/tocochromanol metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported in Supplementary Figure 4 rather than main figures.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant protection and support of chlorophyll/tocopherol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: downregulated during transition from E-L 34 to E-L 38 in berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: decreased transcript abundance accompanies antioxidant pathway remodeling; still positively correlated with sterol and glycerolipids in network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvGGR expression declined during berry ripening. 2. Network analysis linked VvGGR positively to sterol and glycerolipids. 3. The data suggest coupling between antioxidant side-chain metabolism and lipid status in plastids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Associated with antioxidant-lipid relationship in discussion.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant protection of photosystems and membrane lipids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: downregulated during transition from E-L 34 to E-L 38 in berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduced expression indicates remodeling of plastid antioxidant quinone metabolism during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvVTE3 transcript levels decreased in berry skins from green to mature stage. 2. In network analysis, VvVTE3 positively correlated with two glycerolipids. 3. The data support coupling of tocopherol/plastoquinone metabolism with plastid lipid composition.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supplementary figure-based evidence.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photoprotection and membrane lipid protection from photooxidation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated during transition from E-L 34 to E-L 38 in berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: upregulation at maturity is associated with increased tocopherol/quinone accumulation and persistent photoprotective antioxidant capacity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvVTE4 was upregulated in mature berry skins. 2. Its induction correlated with higher tocopherol and quinone levels in mature plastids. 3. The results support continued antioxidant protection of photosystem II and membranes in mature berry skins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted quinone/tocochromanol metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: VvVTE4 connected major network clusters per results/discussion.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photoprotection and membrane lipid protection
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; notable hub in network
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated during transition from E-L 34 to E-L 38 according to discussion and network section
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher expression at maturity is associated with increased tocopherol accumulation and acts as a major positive network hub
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvVTE1 was highlighted as upregulated from green to mature berry skins. 2. It positively correlated with 21 metabolites in the largest network cluster. 3. Discussion links VvVTE1 induction with higher tocopherol accumulation in mature berry skin plastids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Though detailed fold change is not given in the main text, the gene is emphasized as a major hub.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: membrane lipid biosynthesis and plastid development
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point/committed step of fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: increased by 111% in berry skins at E-L 38
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: induction at maturity suggests sustained or remodeled fatty acid precursor synthesis despite overall decline of many plastid lipids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvACCase transcripts increased during berry ripening. 2. It was a major hub positively correlated with 21 isoprenoid and lipid metabolites. 3. The data indicate central coordination between fatty acid precursor synthesis and plastid metabolic remodeling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted lipid metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted metabolomics; qPCR; untargeted metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although many lipids declined, ACCase transcript increased, indicating complex regulation beyond transcript abundance.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: provides precursor for lipid biosynthesis and aroma-related fatty acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: activates acetate to acetyl-CoA, supplying carbon for fatty acid and related metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: downregulated by 88% in berry skins at E-L 38; in leaves reduced by 84%
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in green berry skins and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strong downregulation accompanies decline of many plastid lipids during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvACS1 expression decreased strongly in berry skins and leaves during development. 2. It clustered with genes from chlorophyll, carotenoid, and tocopherol pathways, suggesting shared regulation. 3. The decline is consistent with reduced lipid biosynthetic activity in mature plastids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; lipid metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named as ACS1 in some sections and VvACS1 in others.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: membrane and storage lipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: acylates glycerol-3-phosphate to initiate glycerolipid biosynthesis leading to DAG and TAG formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: no significant difference in berry skins; reduced by 73% in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: stable in berry skins across ripening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: stable expression in berry skins despite lipid declines suggests downstream or post-transcriptional control of glycerolipid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvGPAT did not significantly change expression in berry skins between E-L 34 and E-L 38. 2. In leaves, expression decreased strongly during development. 3. Discussion notes GPAT grouped with genes from several plastidial pathways, implying shared control networks.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted lipid profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion refers to VvGPAT1; main text lists VvGPAT.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: neutral lipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: adds an acyl group to DAG to form TAG in the final step of TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: ER membrane/possibly plastid-associated contamination in fractions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: no significant difference in berry skins between E-L 34 and E-L 38
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: expression remains stable despite major changes in lipid profile; positively correlated with campesterol in network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvDGAT transcript abundance was unchanged during berry ripening. 2. In network analysis, VvDGAT correlated positively with campesterol. 3. Lipid remodeling in mature berry skin plastids therefore cannot be explained by DGAT transcript changes alone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; targeted lipid profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Some measured lipids may partly reflect non-plastid contamination, as noted by authors.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chlorophyll turnover and senescence-related dismantling of photosystems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces chlorophyll b during conversion to chlorophyll a in chlorophyll degradation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated by 49% in berry skins at E-L 38; unchanged in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enhanced transcript abundance at maturity suggests activation of chlorophyll turnover without net chloroplast loss in berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvNYC1 expression increased moderately in mature berry skins. 2. The increase, together with strong SGR induction, indicates activation of chlorophyll degradation machinery. 3. In network analysis, VvNYC1 positively correlated with VvPDS1 and two glycerolipids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; chlorophyll metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although degradative, it is experimentally profiled as a core pathway gene in the study.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dismantling photosynthetic complexes and initiating chlorophyll breakdown
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: pheophytin a/b or demetallated chlorophyll intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: promotes dismantling of chlorophyll-protein complexes and magnesium removal to initiate chlorophyll breakdown
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key initiation step of chlorophyll breakdown
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated by 403% in berry skins at E-L 38 and by 1400% in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: strongly induced in mature berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strong induction indicates activation of chlorophyll breakdown machinery during ripening, though chlorophyll levels remain relatively stable in berry skin plastids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvSGR was strongly induced in mature berry skins and even more strongly in leaves. 2. Discussion identifies SGR as required for dismantling photosynthetic chlorophyll-protein complexes and initiating chlorophyll breakdown. 3. The data suggest degradation machinery is activated earlier/more strongly in leaves than in berry skins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR transcript profiling; chlorophyll metabolite profiling; literature-supported functional interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not in network hub list, SGR is functionally emphasized in discussion as central to chlorophyll breakdown.
